    protected static int[] uncompressHeadless(SkippableIntegerCODEC codec, int[] data, int len) {
        int[] outBuf = new int[len + 1024];
        IntWrapper inPos = IntWrapper.IntWrapper1();
        IntWrapper outPos = IntWrapper.IntWrapper1();
        codec.headlessUncompress(data, inPos, data.length, outBuf, outPos,len);
        if(outPos.get() < len) throw new RuntimeException("Insufficient output.");
        return Arrays.copyOf(outBuf, outPos.get());
    }
```

    protected static int[] uncompressHeadless(SkippableIntegerCODEC codec, int[] data, int len) {
        int[] outBuf = new int[len + 1024];
        IntWrapper inPos = IntWrapper.IntWrapper1();
        IntWrapper outPos = IntWrapper.IntWrapper1();
        codec.headlessUncompress(data, inPos, data.length, outBuf, outPos,len);
        if(outPos.get() < len) throw new RuntimeException("Insufficient output.");
        return Arrays.copyOf(outBuf, outPos.get());
    }
```

```python
def uncompressHeadless(codec, data: List[int], len: int) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, int]:
    outBuf = np.zeros(len + 1024, dtype=int)
    inPos = 0
    outPos = 0
    codec.headlessUncompress(data, inPos, len(data), outBuf, outPos, len)
    if outPos < len: raise RuntimeError("Insufficient output.")
    return (outBuf[:outPos], outPos)
```

The output is a tuple containing a numpy array (equivalent to Java's int[]) and an integer, which is represented by the type `Tuple[np.ndarray, int]` in Python.
